summary: Proof-worth registry entries owned by voice-io.

entries.contract: Voice daemon-event fixtures and the frozen voice event vocabulary stay aligned with the canonical daemon event schema.
entries.worth: This catches voice wake and dictation envelope drift before renderer or daemon behavior tests consume invalid events.

entries.contract: Sigil renderer dictation state emits key-driven voice envelopes and consumes generic daemon voice envelopes without re-emitting them.
entries.worth: This proves the renderer voice bridge without needing a live daemon, keyboard hook, or status item.
entries.guard: renderer module unit; no live daemon

entries.contract: Sigil voice response policy, voice runtime, status-menu runtime, and main renderer preserve their event and menu delegation boundaries.
entries.worth: This catches voice response behavior or ownership drift without requiring a live microphone, daemon, or speech provider.
entries.guard: renderer module unit; no live daemon or speech provider

entries.contract: Sigil voice runtime arbitrates hold-Space input ownership, preserves active dictation release, and coordinates dictation and response state through injected renderer context.
entries.worth: This catches input-priority and state-stranding regressions without requiring a live input tap, microphone, daemon, or speech provider.
entries.guard: renderer module unit; no live daemon or input tap

entries.contract: Voice bind supports exact, filtered, unavailable, disabled, and unspeakable voice assignment outcomes.
entries.worth: This catches assignment and allocatability regressions using mock providers in an isolated state root.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT and mock voice providers

entries.contract: Voice session allocation rotates through mock voices, preserves cursor state, and revalidates assignments after policy changes.
entries.worth: This proves rotation and policy-filter behavior without touching the shared repo daemon.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT and mock voice providers

entries.contract: Voice subcommands reject unknown flags and missing values with the external command error contract.
entries.worth: This keeps voice CLI forms aligned with maintainer-facing parser semantics.

entries.contract: Voice final-response hooks route Codex and Claude payloads through the human voice response policy with disabled-delivery acknowledgement.
entries.worth: This protects final response speech routing without requiring a live TTS provider.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT; voice delivery remains disabled

entries.contract: Voice IDs round-trip, preserve provider namespaces, canonicalize system IDs, and fail closed on malformed inputs.
entries.worth: This prevents ambiguous provider IDs from leaking into assignment, policy, or speech paths.

entries.contract: Voice policy file rewrites are observed by the daemon and reflected in voice availability and session assignment state.
entries.worth: This catches policy watcher and reload regressions using a disposable state root.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT and mock voice providers

entries.contract: Voice providers list exposes the canonical system and ElevenLabs provider catalog shape.
entries.worth: This catches provider registry regressions before voice assignment or speech paths consume the catalog.

entries.contract: The internal voice registry snapshot exposes sorted mock voices and parser errors through the external error contract.
entries.worth: This gives the cheapest deterministic proof that mock voice fixtures stay discoverable for voice tests.
entries.guard: uses mock provider registry without daemon startup

entries.contract: Voice session allocation assigns, persists, restores, and rebinds mock voices across daemon restarts.
entries.worth: This protects session voice continuity without using the shared repo runtime.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT and mock voice providers

entries.contract: Voice assignment fallback emits telemetry when stored session preferences are no longer allocatable.
entries.worth: This catches silent policy and allocation failures that would otherwise look like successful session restore.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT and mock voice providers
